title: Silas (Mandalorian)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Silas}} {{Character |type=Mandalorian |image=[[File:Silas_Closeup.jpg]] |name=Silas |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],This date is derived from simple mathematical deductions using the time markers provided in [[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']] in relation to the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], which occurs in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. [[Kohlma]]Although ''Open Seasons'' does not specify the location of [[Dooku's castle (Kohlma)|Dooku's Lair]], ''[[The Unseen Planets of Episode II]]'' in [[Star Wars Insider 80|''Star Wars Insider'' 80]] depicts it as being located on [[Kohlma]] in the [[Bogden system/Legends|Bogden system]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Green |skin=Fair |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] **[[True Mandalorians]]Although Silas's single appearance in [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] does not make a specific reference to the term "[[True Mandalorians]]," both ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' confirm that the commandos who followed [[Jaster Mereel|Mereel]] and [[Jango Fett/Legends|Fett]] during the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]] were of the True Mandalorian faction. |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'll follow Jango. And no one else.|Silas|}} '''Silas''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] during the final [[Standard year/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He was a rookie in the [[True Mandalorians|True Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Army/Legends|army]] around [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], when he joined his friend and mentor [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] in the [[Battle of Korda Six]]. The operation on [[Korda Six]] was led by [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Jaster Mereel]], with the objective of extracting members of the [[Korda Defense Force]] from hostile territory; however, the mission was actually a trap for Mereel and his men, set by the Mandalorian renegades known as [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]. The violent splinter group decimated the Korda extraction teams and [[Death/Legends|murdered]] Mereel during the ensuing battle. Silas and Fett watched as Mereel's comrade, [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]], left him to die on the battlefield so that he might assume command of the Mandalorian armies. When Montross made his bid for control, Silas instead stood behind Fett and insisted that he become the new [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] in Mereel's stead. Silas's wishes were echoed by the other surviving Mandalorians, who [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] Montross and took Fett as their new leader. Silas served under Fett up until the debacle on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]], where members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] [[Battle of Galidraan|wiped out]] the remaining True Mandalorian mercenaries. Both men survived the event, making them the last two True Mandalorians of [[Supercommando Codex|Mereel's discipline]]. [[32 BBY/Legends|Twelve years later]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]] took an interest in Fett; he kidnapped Silas and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] the man in an attempt to extract information from him on the Mandalore's background. The Mandalorian eventually succumbed to Tyranus's overtures and told him the history of the battle at Korda Six. Although Silas offered to provide more information, Tyranus refused and had the Mandalorian killed. ==Biography== ===Brothers in arms=== {{Quote|Montross disappeared, and Jango became our leader. He became Jaster's legacy…|Silas, recounting the aftermath of Korda Six|}} [[File:Jango_and_Silas.jpg|thumb|left|280px|Fett {{C|left}} and Silas {{C|right}} during their time as mercenaries under Jaster Mereel]] Silas was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the latter [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].According to {{CSWECite|I|363|Great Peace of the Republic}}, the "Great Peace of the Republic" ends with the onset of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] takes place [[32 BBY/Legends|one decade prior]] to the Clone Wars. At some point after [[58 BBY/Legends|58 BBY]], Silas joined the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Army/Legends|armies]], at the [[Time/Legends|time]] led by [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Jaster Mereel]]. The young man endured the traditional [[Verd'goten|training]] of a Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] to become a [[Mandalorian Supercommando|supercommando]] under Mereel's [[Supercommando Codex]].Although Silas's single appearance in [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] does not make a specific reference to the [[Supercommando Codex]], ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']] in [[Star Wars Insider 80|''Star Wars Insider'' 80]] confirm that the commandos who followed [[Jaster Mereel]] during the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]] operated under this doctrine. During that time, he befriended the Mandalore's young protégé, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], who had joined the warrior clans sometime prior to Silas's own induction. Silas grew to greatly admire Fett and supported the man's ascension through the Mandalorian command structure. In [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], the Mandalorians were contacted by the [[Korda Defense Force]] of [[Korda Six]] in a request for aid. Mereel accepted the job and formed a task force led by himself, Fett, and veteran [[Commando/Legends|commando]] [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]]. Silas was assigned to serve under Fett during the operation, which marked Fett's first solo command of his [[Squad/Legends|squad]], [[Jango's grunts|Jango's Grunts]]. During a briefing on the mission, Mereel explained to his commandos that the mission's objective was to locate and extract a rookie security team from hostile territory on Korda Six, which, according to the reconnaissance provided by the Korda Defense Force, was being lightly defended by [[Kordan]] forces with little defensive organization. Silas and the rest of Jango's Grunts were to provide covering fire for Montross's [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]-equipped [[Vertigo Company|Vertigo Squad]] and keep the landing zone clear while Mereel led his [[Headhunter Company|Headhunter Squad]] to retrieve the security team. The commandos boarded [[Meteor-class Q-Carrier|''Meteor''-class Q-Carrier]] [[Starship/Legends|vessels]]{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} and made their way for Kordan [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], where they discovered a reality very different from the fictionalized reconnaissance provided by the Kordan Defense Force. The ships quickly came under fire from [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] installments on the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] surface, forcing many of the [[Carrier/Legends|carriers]], including Silas's, to execute emergency crash landings. When Silas and the men of Jango's Grunts disembarked from their downed craft, they immediately came under fire from Kordan fortifications on a ridge above the crash site. The Mandalorian forces took heavy losses as they scrambled to find cover, but Fett and Silas were able to lead their squad into one of the trenches formed by the trajectories of the downed [[Dropship/Legends|drop ships]]. The men were quickly followed by Headhunter Squad and Mereel, who decreed that the mission was a failure and that all forces were to abort their objectives and return to the dropships. The order was disregarded by Montross, who led his Vertigo Squad in a brazen attack run on the ridge. While the Mandalore left to retrieve his wayward subordinate, Fett and Silas took the three other members of Jango's Grunts to rescue the security rookies at the extraction site. [[File:Jango_and_Silas_Korda.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Silas prevents Fett from running into a crossfire.]] Upon discovering the empty clearing that should have been the location of the rookie security team, Jango's Grunts were ambushed by members of the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]—a renegade faction of Mandalorians who had split from Mereel's forces shortly before Silas joined the warrior clans. The Death Watch forces pursued the [[True Mandalorians]] through the Kordan forest, and eventually [[Death/Legends|killed]] every member of the Grunts save for Fett and Silas. The two men were able to escape their attackers when Fett incinerated them with his [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrower]]. They concluded that the entire job had been a Death Watch setup—the renegades had armed and trained the locals and then planted the false reconnaissance. As Fett contacted Mereel to inform him of the development, he and Silas trekked back toward the battlefield to rendezvous with their [[Commander/Legends|commander]] and depart the planet. Upon reaching the combat zone, the two bore witness to the Mandalore being gunned down by Death Watch leader [[Tor Vizsla]] while Montross fled the scene, leaving his comrade to die alone. Fett attempted to run into the crossfire to save Mereel and was nearly killed in the attempt, but he was instead saved by Silas, who threw him to the ground before the [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire could claim Fett's life as well. After the Death Watch and Kordan forces withdrew from the area, Fett and Silas approached Mereel's inert body. Silas watched as Fett bid his final farewell to his mentor and then followed the man back to the landing zone, where they found Montross claiming that Fett had died trying to save the Mandalore. The two interrupted Montross's attempts to usurp control of the Mandalorian forces in the wake of Mereel's death, revealing to their comrades that he had abandoned their commander on the field of battle. Fett rejected Montross's insinuation that he should be the new Mandalore, and Silas stood behind him, drawing his blaster on Montross and affirming that he would be led by no one other than Jango. Their fellow Mandalorians followed suit, and Montross was forced into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] while Fett assumed leadership and led his commandos off Korda 6. For the next eight years, Silas and the True Mandalorians followed Fett in his [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|war]] against Vizsla and the Death Watch. In [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], the Mandalorians were contacted by the [[Governor of Galidraan|governor]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]], who claimed that the Death Watch leader and his men were camped out on his planet. The [[Governor/Legends|governor]] agreed to reveal Vizsla's location, provided that Fett and his mercenaries first destroy a cell of [[Galidraanian rebels|political activists]] who were creating unrest for the Galidraan regime. Fett agreed and carried out the governor's wishes before confronting the man for the information on Vizsla. The Galidraan operation ultimately turned out to be a setup, just like Korda Six, and Fett was ambushed by the Death Watch in the [[Galidraan governor's palace|governor's palace]]. Meanwhile, the remainder of the True Mandalorian mercenaries were confronted by members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], who had been summoned to the world under the pretense that the Mandalorians had been slaughtering innocent civilians. The battle with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] resulted in the destruction of the True Mandalorians,[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 3|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 3]] with Fett and Silas being the only surviving members.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ===After the war=== {{Quote|This can all end in moments. You know what I want.''"
"''You just want me to betray the greatest Mandalorian who ever lived…twenty years ago…I almost died for Jango Fett.|Darth Tyranus succeeds in breaking Silas|}} [[File:Dooku_and_Silas.jpg|thumb|left|330px|Tyranus interrogates Silas.]] In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Silas was kidnapped by [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]], a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], who held him within his [[Dooku's castle (Kohlma)|private retreat]] on the burial [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Kohlma]] in the [[Bogden system/Legends|Bogden system]]. Tyranus was researching Jango Fett's history, as the Mandalorian had made a name for himself as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] in the aftermath of Galidraan, and had since attracted the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord's]] attention. Silas's old comrade had been lured into a [[The Hunt|bounty hunting contest]] arranged by Tyranus, and was the expected victor. Because the contest's [[Clone template/Legends|spoils]] were so important to the plans of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], Tyranus hoped to collect extensive intelligence on the Mandalorian bounty hunter before entering into an agreement with the man.[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]] In the dungeons of the lair, Silas was [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] at the command of Tyranus with a number of [[Interrogation droid/Legends|interrogation droids]] and a [[scan grid]], in the hopes of enticing the man to reveal Fett's history. As a Mandalorian, Silas had been trained to withstand torture, but he eventually succumbed when injected with the powerful truth serum{{CSWECite|I|65|Bavo Six}} [[Bavo Six]]. Upon learning that his [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] had relented, Tyranus visited the dungeons to listen to Silas's admissions. The broken Mandalorian revealed to the Sith the carnage that had occurred on Korda Six and Fett's rise to power. Although he offered to provide Tyranus with an accounting of the massacre on Galidraan, his captor refused—the Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] had previously been known as Dooku, the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who led Jedi Order's task force during the mission to Galidraan. Understanding that Silas would yield no more useful information, Tyranus ordered his [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to stop the Mandalorian's [[Heart/Legends|heart]] functions. Silas's last wish was that the Sith Lord not inform Fett of his complicity; however, Tyranus refused to promise as much. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Please, don't tell Jango…''"
"''I can't make any promises. Goodbye, Silas.|Darth Tyranus denies Silas's final wish|}} [[File:Jango_Silas_Montross.jpg|thumb|right|190px|Silas defends Fett, securing the latter's position as the new Mandalore.]] Silas's career as a Mandalorian and his death at the hands of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Tyranus were characterized by his devotion to his Mandalore, Jango Fett. Silas greatly admired Fett and considered him to be the greatest Mandalorian to have ever existed in the order's seven thousand-year history.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' The two men became friendly during Silas's early years in the warrior clans, when Fett was still serving as a low-level commando. Silas supported his friend's ascension to command and eagerly served under him during the battle on Korda Six. On Korda, Silas saved Fett's life by preventing him from running into oncoming blaster fire, and watched as the man bid farewell to his adoptive father, Jaster Mereel. When Montross tried to assume command of the Mandalorian forces, Silas was the first to draw his weapon on the usurper and nominated Fett for the role of the new Mandalore. The sentiment was shared by their comrades, and Montross's attempt was foiled. It was Silas's strong relationship with Fett that ultimately attracted the attention of Tyranus twenty years later, when the Sith Lord was attempting to glean information on the Mandalore from the man's former colleagues. Although he had been trained by the Mandalorians to anticipate a myriad of eventualities, including torture, Silas eventually caved in to Tyranus's demands, albeit regretfully. He felt that indulging the Sith Lord was tantamount to betraying his old friend, for whom he had nearly sacrificed his life on Korda Six. Despite his disappointment in himself for telling Tyranus of the bloodshed on Korda, Silas still offered to go into detail about the massacre on Galidraan. When the Sith refused and ordered that his prisoner be executed, Silas used his final words to ask that Fett not be informed of his failure. Silas had light [[Color/Legends|green]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], dark brown hair, and fair skin. During the mission on Korda 6, the young mercenary donned [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] painted green and blue. In Mandalorian culture, the colors symbolized duty and reliability, respectively.''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' ==Behind the scenes== Silas was first seen in the [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|second installment]] of [[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']], a [[2002]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series that told the history of the Mandalorian Jango Fett through flashback sequences. All four issues of ''Open Seasons'' were written by [[W. Haden Blackman]] and penciled by [[Ramón F. Bachs]]. Silas was again seen in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', as ''Open Seasons 2'' was available for viewing in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] via unlockable bonus content. He later received an entry in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' {{C|Reprint of ''Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2''}} *[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Silas (Mandalorianer) |pl=Silas |pt=Legends:Silas }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:True Mandalorians]]
